title: Logic Gates and Boolean Algebra
summary: Logic gates implement Boolean operations in hardware. Boolean algebra provides the mathematical tools to analyse, simplify, and design gate networks.
content_html:
Boolean Algebra Basics

Boolean algebra operates on binary values (0 and 1) using three fundamental operations:

OperationSymbolDescriptionExample
AND·Output 1 only if all inputs are 11·1=1, 1·0=0
OR+Output 1 if any input is 11+0=1, 0+0=0
NOTā or a'Invert the inputNOT 1 = 0

Derived Gates

GateExpressionKey property
NANDNOT(A·B)Universal gate — any function from NAND alone
NORNOT(A+B)Universal gate — any function from NOR alone
XORA⊕BOutput 1 when inputs differ; basis of addition
XNORNOT(A⊕B)Output 1 when inputs are equal

De Morgan's Laws

NOT(A·B) = Ā + B̄     NOT(A+B) = Ā · B̄

These laws let you transform NAND↔OR-with-inverted-inputs and NOR↔AND-with-inverted-inputs.

Boolean Identities

IdentityANDOR
IdentityA·1 = AA+0 = A
NullA·0 = 0A+1 = 1
ComplementA·Ā = 0A+Ā = 1
IdempotentA·A = AA+A = A
Double NOTNOT(NOT A) = A

Gate Design Example

Design a circuit for output F = A·B + C·D:

  1. AND gate 1: computes A·B
  2. AND gate 2: computes C·D
  3. OR gate: combines A·B and C·D
Simplification: Boolean algebra allows simplification of complex expressions before building circuits, reducing gate count and power consumption.
